user_query:
I need a phosphorus (P) fertilization planning sheet for our 6 fields. Read input/fields.csv and produce two files:

1) output/recommendations.csv with these exact header columns:
- FieldID
- SoilTestMethod
- SoilTestP_ppm
- Category
- Action
- PlannedCrop
- ExpectedYield
- RemovalRate_P2O5_lb_per_unit
- Maintenance_P2O5_lb_acre
- Notes

Rules:
- Determine Category using the soil test interpretation thresholds appropriate to the listed SoilTestMethod (Bray, Olsen, or Mehlich 3).
- Set Action exactly to one of: "Heavy P needed", "Build P level", "Maintenance", "Reduce/skip P", or "No P needed" according to the Category from the soil test interpretation table.
- Compute Maintenance_P2O5_lb_acre as ExpectedYield multiplied by the crop’s P2O5 removal rate. Use agronomic crop removal rates (lb P2O5 per yield unit) consistent with standard references for these crops.
- RemovalRate_P2O5_lb_per_unit must match the rate used to compute Maintenance_P2O5_lb_acre.
- Round Maintenance_P2O5_lb_acre to 2 decimal places.
- In Notes, add a brief placement/timing suggestion (one phrase).

2) output/summary.json containing:
- total_fields (integer)
- categories (object with keys: "Very Low", "Low", "Medium", "High", "Very High" and integer counts)
- total_maintenance_P2O5 (sum of Maintenance_P2O5_lb_acre across all fields as a number)

Assumptions:
- Use method-specific soil test P interpretation thresholds for Bray, Olsen, and Mehlich 3.
- Use standard P2O5 crop removal factors for: Corn (grain), Soybean, Wheat, Alfalfa hay, Corn silage, and Potato.
- Do not hardcode anything outside what you can justify from agronomic references.

Write the two outputs only under output/ paths specified above.
